title: ProtocolLab Runner v1
purpose: Define the ProtocolLab Runner v1 orchestration contract before implementation changes are made.
scope: This specification covers manifest catalog loading, suite resolution, run-cell planning, compatibility filtering, target lifecycle, readiness, validation gating, load-tool invocation, artifact capture, result classification, and report writing. Raw QUIC execution, Incursa protocol adapters, WebTransport, MASQUE, database workloads, nginx, Caddy, quic-go, and network simulation remain outside this runner definition until separately scheduled.
context: ProtocolLab is a local validation and benchmarking harness. The runner coordinates implementations through manifests, execution backend contracts, ports, commands, and artifacts while keeping protocol-specific behavior behind validators, load tools, and execution backends.

requirements.title: Manifest catalog loading
requirements.statement: The runner MUST load implementation, scenario, suite, load tool, and network profile manifests into a validated catalog before plan expansion.

requirements.title: Deterministic plan expansion
requirements.statement: The runner MUST expand selected suites and options into deterministic run cells with stable cell identities and artifact paths.

requirements.title: Compatibility classification
requirements.statement: The runner MUST classify unsupported, unavailable, incompatible, and runnable cells before target startup.

requirements.title: Execution backend abstraction
requirements.statement: The runner MUST start targets through execution backend contracts selected from manifests.

requirements.title: Target lifecycle cleanup
requirements.statement: The runner MUST stop and clean up target resources after each runnable cell attempt.

requirements.title: Readiness waiting
requirements.statement: The runner MUST wait for manifest-defined target readiness before validation or load execution.

requirements.title: Readiness failure reporting
requirements.statement: The runner MUST record readiness timeout or startup failure as a cell result with preserved diagnostics.

requirements.title: Validation gate
requirements.statement: The runner MUST validate each runnable cell before accepting benchmark measurements.

requirements.title: Failed validation handling
requirements.statement: The runner MUST mark failed, unsupported, or unavailable validation as non-accepted benchmark data.

requirements.title: Load tool invocation
requirements.statement: The runner MUST invoke load tools through manifest-defined process, Docker, or managed execution definitions.

requirements.title: Raw output preservation
requirements.statement: The runner MUST preserve raw load-tool stdout and stderr for every benchmark attempt.

requirements.title: Deterministic artifact layout
requirements.statement: The runner MUST write run, cell, validation, target, load-tool, and report artifacts to deterministic paths under `.artifacts/runs/{runId}`.

requirements.title: Aggregate machine-readable results
requirements.statement: The runner MUST produce aggregate machine-readable results covering every planned cell.

requirements.title: Human-readable summaries
requirements.statement: The runner MUST produce a human-readable summary for each completed run.

requirements.title: Evidence and comparability classification
requirements.statement: The runner MUST classify result evidence quality and comparability independently of protocol-specific assumptions.

requirements.title: No direct protocol implementation coupling
requirements.statement: The runner MUST NOT reference Incursa protocol implementation assemblies from runner or CLI orchestration assemblies.

requirements.title: Runner result contract
requirements.statement: The runner MUST return structured command results that include command kind, exit code, messages, and artifact references.

requirements.title: Presentation separation
requirements.statement: The runner MUST keep console rendering and command-line presentation outside the runner project.

requirements.title: Fixture catalog and cell coverage
requirements.statement: The runner MUST provide fake implementation manifests, fake scenarios, fake suites, and fake labs that expand into successful, unsupported, and incompatible cells without real protocol behavior.

requirements.title: Fixture backend and lifecycle outcomes
requirements.statement: The runner MUST provide fake execution backends and in-memory target lifecycle behavior that can produce startup failure, readiness timeout, readiness success, and cleanup outcomes deterministically.

requirements.title: Fixture validation and load-tool outcomes
requirements.statement: The runner MUST provide fake validators and fake load tools that can succeed, fail validation, fail execution, and fail metric parsing while preserving raw stdout and stderr.

requirements.title: Fixture artifact and report generation
requirements.statement: The runner MUST produce deterministic cell artifacts, aggregate results, and reports for successful and failing fixture cells.

requirements.title: Direct runner host proof
requirements.statement: The runner MUST be invocable directly through RunnerEngine and prove structured result and event behavior without console coupling.

requirements.title: CLI host smoke proof
requirements.statement: The runner MUST be invocable through command host smoke usage so the command host can render runner output while preserving runner exit codes.

requirements.title: Fixture documentation surface
requirements.statement: The runner documentation MUST cover the runner overview, quickstart, manifest reference, scenario contract, execution backend contract, load tool contract, artifact layout, result model, and fixture lab usage.
